rules.title: BukkitScheduler call (throws on Folia)
rules.detail: org.bukkit.scheduler.BukkitScheduler methods throw UnsupportedOperationException on Folia. Tier 1 redirects these to the shim scheduler facade.

rules.title: Scheduler lookup (Bukkit/Server.getScheduler)
rules.detail: Acquiring the BukkitScheduler is harmless on Folia — only using it throws. The uses are redirected by Tier 1; the lookup stays in place.

rules.title: BukkitRunnable scheduling/cancel
rules.detail: BukkitRunnable.runTask*/cancel/getTaskId rely on the Bukkit scheduler. Tier 1 redirects these call sites to shim helpers with a runnable-to-task registry.

rules.title: Bukkit.isPrimaryThread() (semantics change on Folia)
rules.detail: Folia has many region threads instead of one primary thread. The shim provides a region-ownership-aware replacement.

rules.title: Bukkit.getCurrentTick() (throws off a region thread on Folia)
rules.detail: org.bukkit.Bukkit.getCurrentTick() throws IllegalStateException ('No currently ticking region') when called off a region thread — e.g. from an async event handler. Tier 1 redirects it to a shim that returns the real tick when available and a continuous wall-clock estimate otherwise.

rules.title: Entity potion-effect change (needs the entity's region thread on Folia)
rules.detail: addPotionEffect/addPotionEffects/removePotionEffect mutate an entity. On Folia these throw a main-thread check when run off the entity's region (e.g. from a global-scheduler task or async thread). Tier 1 routes them through the entity's scheduler when the current thread doesn't own the entity.

rules.title: Teleport call (may require teleportAsync on Folia)
rules.detail: Matched by name+signature because the call-site owner is the concrete receiver type (Player, LivingEntity, ...). Region-crossing teleports must be async on Folia; routed through the shim teleport helper in Tier 1.

rules.title: Uses Minecraft server internals (NMS) — version-sensitive
rules.detail: Calls into net.minecraft.* classes, which change with every Minecraft version and are not covered by any API compatibility promise. The converter cannot port these; if the plugin targets a different Minecraft version than your server, these calls are the likeliest thing to break.

rules.title: Uses CraftBukkit internals — version-sensitive
rules.detail: Calls into org.bukkit.craftbukkit.* implementation classes (often via reflection on the versioned package name). These are version-specific server internals; plugins built for older Minecraft frequently crash here on modern servers.
